penalized_rmse.note: Miss penalty = matching tolerance, not full search range. A missed path is 'at least one tolerance away' from any estimate.

dictionary_coherence.mu_far_excludes: local NMS neighbourhood around each column

oracle_nmse_naming.nmse_oracle_support_ls: {τ,ν}_true + α̂_LS — isolates gain estimation error Δ_gain
oracle_nmse_naming.nmse_estimated_support_ls: {τ̂,ν̂}_DD + α̂_LS — isolates support estimation error Δ_support

gate_pass_reference.note: A research decision threshold, not a universal theorem.

gate_0_conclusion.summary: Gate 0-A (Known-K identifiability): CONDITIONAL PASS. Under known path count K=4 and fixed Top-K output, Random pilots with density >= 1/8 and SNR >= ~5 dB recover ~84-88% of true path power and identify ~65-74% of true paths. At density 1/4, power recovery reaches ~87-90%. The DD estimator stably captures dominant channel energy. Gate 0-B (Unknown-K detection) remains as future work.
limitations: Fixed Top-(K=4) output — recall == precision by construction
No unknown-path-count detection or stopping rule
Complex-gain accuracy not yet validated (see Gate 1)
Power recovery > 0.8 does not imply accurate TF channel NMSE

main.rationale: Judge whether Oracle DD + complex-gain token has clear value.

boundary.rationale: Test whether physical support helps under low-quality pilots.

stress.rationale: Test whether Gate 1 benefits persist at low pilot density.
